rulings.Case Title: DM 2465 VAN NESS HOLDINGS MEMEBER INVESTOR LLC VS. NAVID SARRAFZADEH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Enforcement Of Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 918.5
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2025, line 9, RESPONDENT NAVID SARRAFZADEH'S Motion To Stay Enforcement Of Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 918.5 Respondent and judgment debtor Navid Sarrafzadeh's motion to stay enforcement of judgment pursuant to CCP 918.5 is denied. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DM 2465 VAN NESS HOLDINGS MEMEBER INVESTOR LLC VS. NAVID SARRAFZADEH
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2025, line9, 1 - PETITIONER DM 2465 VAN NESS HOLDINGS MEMEBER INVESTOR LLC'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION Exemptions from the collection of a judgment are statutory. (In re Silverman's Estate (1967) 249 Cal.App.2d 180, 184.) CCP 704.220(a) provides that "[m]oney in the judgment debtor's deposit account in an amount equal to or less than the minimum basic standard of adequate care for a family of four for Region 1, established by Section 11452 of the Welfare and Institutions Code and as annually adjusted by the State Department of Social Services pursuant to Section 11453 of the Welfare and Institutions Code, is exempt without making a claim." CCP 704.225 permits the court to exempt additional monies upon a claim "to the extent necessary for the support of the judgment debtor and the spouse and dependents of the judgment debtor." The court does not credit Judgment Debtor's financial declaration as a true showing of necessity; it shows expenses of $26,970 on income of $5,000. In particular, the school and childcare expenses for a single child and the transportation expenses are excessive. Nonetheless in recognition of Judgment Debtor's caregiving responsibilities the court will exempt an additional $1000 in addition to the exempt amount available without a claim, $2170 for a family of four pursuant ot CCP 704.220(a), from the Bank of America account. (The court takes judicial notice of DSS's current MBSAC calculation.) The total exempt amount is $3170 from the Bank of America account. The claim of exemption is otherwise denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TOWERVIEW SF II, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. BARTHOLOMEW MURPHY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants (1) Vanguard Properties, Inc., (2) Andrew Warren, And (3) Frank Nolan To Further Respond To Requests For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2025, line8, PLAINTIFF TOWERVIEW SF II, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Defendants (1) Vanguard Properties, Inc., (2) Andrew Warren, And (3) Frank Nolan To Further Respond To Requests For Production Of Documents Disclosure: Approximately 10 years ago, Judge Van Aken and her husband were represented by defendant Andrew Warren in making a purchase offer on a property. The purchase did not go through. Warren showed Judge Van Aken one or two other properties in the intervening years but not more recently than five years ago. Judge Van Aken has had not had contact with Warren in at least the last five years. These circumstances do not give rise to disqualification (see CCP 170.1) but the court nonetheless discloses them. Tentative Ruling: Plaintiff Towerview SF II LLC's motion to compel is granted in part and denied in part. Towerview has not complied with CRC, Rule 3.1345(a), which requires a separate statement. Towerview contends the court should exercise its discretion to excuse the lack, but a separate statement serves important purposes for the court and it is unwieldy and time-consuming for the court to fully address a motion like this without one. That said, the Vanguard defendants have agreed in their opposition to "serve verified responses from which the Joint Defense Privilege, Notice to Consumer deficiency and Equally Available to Plaintiff objections are removed, and which confirm only documents covered by the attorney client privilege or attorney work product privilege are withheld from production [and] to produce a copy of the applicable insurance policy, redacted for premium information." (Opp. 4:7-13.) The Vanguard defendants are ordered to do so by April 10, 2025 and to provide a privilege log for any documents withheld pursuant to any privilege by the same date. The court finds that both parties' positions in this motion lacked substantial justification, and it does not award discovery sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2025, line3, PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. Health and Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (f)(4) provides: "If there is a settlement of an action brought by a person in the public interest under subdivision (d), the plaintiff shall submit the settlement, other than a voluntary dismissal in which no consideration is received from the defendant, to the court for approval upon noticed motion, and the court may approve the settlement only if the court makes all of the following findings: (A) The warning that is required by the settlement complies with this chapter. (B) The award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law. (C) The penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in paragraph (2) of subdivision (b)." The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. Accordingly the motion is granted and the court will enter a conforming judgment. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: SAMANTHA PRINCE VS. MARSHALL BEHRMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs (Ccp 425.16(C))
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2025, line 4, DEFENDANTS MARSHALL BEHRMAN , AN INDIVIDUAL, SHARLENE BEHRMAN'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs (Ccp 425.16(C)) Defendants Marshall and Sharlene Behrman succeeded on a special motion to strike Plaintiff Samantha Prince's complaint pursuant to CCP sec. 425.16. They now seek fees and costs in the amount of $50,797. Their motion is supported by a declaration from their counsel that states a number of hours (77.6) and a rate ($650 per hour), but does not contain details about billing or hours spent. For the reasons stated herein, the court grants the motion but reduces the amount awarded according to its lodestar analysis to $13,357. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. rulings.Case Title: GIOVANNA FABIANI-BEEMAN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2025, line 5, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS CITY AND COUNTY AS SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT'S Motion To Stay Defendant the City and County of San Francisco (City)'s motion ot stay proceedings is granted. "[W]hen a federal action has been filed covering the same subject matter as is involved in a California action, the California court has the discretion but not the obligation to stay the state court action." (Caiafa Prof. Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. Co. (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 800, 804.) " 'In exercising its discretion the court should consider the importance of discouraging multiple litigation designed solely to harass an adverse party, and of avoiding unseemly conflicts with the courts of other jurisdictions. It should also consider whether the rights of the parties can best be determined by the court of the other jurisdiction because of the nature of the subject matter, the availability of witnesses, or the stage to which the proceedings in the other court have already advanced.' (Farmland Irrigation. Co. v. Dopplmaier, supra, 48 Cal.2d at p. 215.) The California Supreme Court also has isolated another critical factor favoring a stay of the state court action in favor of the federal action, a factor which happens to be present in this case-the federal action is pending in California not some other state. (Thomson v. Continental Ins. Co., supra, 66 Cal.2d at p. 747.)" (Caiafa, supra, 15 Cal.App.4th, at p. 804.) Other similar actions have previously been stayed by this court. (See Yan Dec. paras. 2-5 & Exs. A-D.) For the reasons stated in this court's order of September 28, 2023, in case number CGC-22-600135, Kricken v. City & County of San Francisco, the court finds that a stay is appropriate here to avoid multiple litigations and the risk of conflicting judgments. Plaintiff's late-filed opposing brief (which the court exercises its discretion to consider) contends that her case is different because it is based on her religious objections. This contention is unpersuasive; pending federal cases address religious liberty claims. (See Sept. 28, 2023 Order at p. 4.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2025, line 10, 2 - DEFENDANT BREITLING USA'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Defendant Breitling USA, Inc.'s ("Breitling") Demurrer to the Complaint is overruled. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* The court denies defendant's request to take judicial notice of the police report since it was provided with the reply papers and not with the moving papers. rulings.Case Title: JUAN COBOS VS. BREITLING USA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2025, line 10, 1 - DEFENDANT BREITLING USA'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Defendant Breitling USA, Inc.'s ("Breitling") Motion to Strike the Complaint is denied. Plaintiff's allegations that support the intentional torts also support the prayer for exemplary damages. As explained in the companion demurrer, Breitling may be liable for Valencia's conduct. Plaintiff's Ralph Act claim supports the attorney's fees request. (Civil Code sections 51.7, 52(b)(3).) Defendant notes that the fee request is not directly tied to the Ralph Act cause of action. Any ambiguity is minor; any fee request will be resolved by a subsequent motion after the case is adjudicated. rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA SALCEDO ET AL VS. KATHY MUNOZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2025, line6, DEFENDANT KATHY MUNOZ"S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Defendant Kathy Munoz brings a special motion to strike Plaintiffs Victoria Salcedo, Tim Leong, and 520 Live, Inc.'s complaint. Munoz contends that Plaintiffs' lawsuit against her was motivated by a separate action she brought claiming Labor Code violations. Because the Plaintiffs' causes of action do not arise from protected activity, and an anti-SLAPP motion does not lie where they are merely motivated by protected activity, the court denies Munoz's motion. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person.
